steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: Jake lost his job two weeks ago. He told you over coffee — no big deal, he said, he'd figure it out. But today, he gets a bad letter from the building manager. The company is raising his rent.
steps.text_cn: Jake两周前失业了。他喝咖啡时告诉过你，说没什么大不了，他会想办法。但今天，他收到楼管寄来的坏消息：公司要给他涨房租。

steps.instruction: "The company is raising his rent." — What is happening to Jake's apartment?
steps.feedback: Right! 'Raising rent' means the apartment costs more. / 对！'Raising rent' 意思是公寓要花更多钱了。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Raising rent' means they want more money for the apartment. / 'Raising rent' 意思是他们要更多的公寓租金。

steps.speaker: Jake
steps.text: You can't ask for more money now!
steps.text_cn: 你们不能现在要更多钱！

steps.speaker: Dan
steps.text: Yes, I can. You signed the paper. Look at the contract. Section 4.2.
steps.text_cn: 可以。你签了文件。看合同，第4.2条。

steps.instruction: Dan shows Jake a contract. Jake looks scared. You need to help — quick!
steps.feedback: Quick thinking! You stepped in to help your neighbor. / 反应快！你站出来帮邻居了。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Jake needs help with the letter — 'Let me see the letter' is the right move. / Jake需要信件方面的帮助——'Let me see the letter' 是正确的做法。

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: You post the photo in the group chat: "Has anyone seen this before?" Kevin answers fast: "Yeah. That's how they got Ryan out."
steps.text_cn: 你把照片发到群聊里："有人以前见过这个吗？" Kevin很快回复："见过。他们当年就是这样把Ryan赶走的。"

steps.instruction: Kevin says "That's how they got Ryan out." Ryan is Mrs. Henderson's son. What does this mean?
steps.feedback: Yes! The same thing happened to Ryan before — they raised rent until he left. / 对！同样的事以前发生在Ryan身上——他们涨租直到他离开。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Got Ryan out' means they forced him to leave his apartment by raising rent. / 'Got Ryan out' 意思是他们通过涨租逼Ryan离开了公寓。
steps.review_word: The company used the same way to make Ryan leave

options.label: A
options.text: The company used the same way to make Ryan leave

options.label: B
options.text: Ryan closed his door and went to sleep

options.label: C
options.text: Ryan added new furniture to his flat

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text_cn: 这次不行。不能再这样了。

steps.speaker: Kevin
steps.text: I know a good lawyer. He can help.
steps.text_cn: 我认识一个好律师。他能帮忙。

steps.speaker: Lisa
steps.text: I will write a letter to the company.

steps.instruction: Kevin found a lawyer. Lisa will write. Now it's your turn to help Jake. What do you say?
steps.feedback: Supportive and clear! You're helping Jake explain his problem. / 又支持又清晰！你在帮Jake理清问题。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Jake needs to explain the situation — 'Tell me what happened' is how you help. / Jake需要解释情况——'Tell me what happened' 是你帮忙的方式。

options.label: C
options.text: Open the door for me, slowly.

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: Jake — tell me what happened, slowly. I'll explain it to the lawyer.
steps.text_cn: Jake，慢慢告诉我发生了什么。我来解释给律师听。

steps.speaker: Jake
steps.text: Thank you. You're the only person I can hear right now.
steps.text_cn: 谢谢你。现在我唯一听得进去的人就是你。

steps.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
steps.speech_text: Match these words: person, people, neighbor.
steps.feedback: Great! 'Person' is one, 'people' is many. / 很好！'Person' 是一个人，'people' 是很多人。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Person = 一个人, people = 很多人, neighbor = 邻居。

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: The lawyer helps Jake. Three days later, the company says: "Okay, no more rent increase." Jake is happy. He sends a message to the group: "4B just saved my apartment! 🎉"
steps.text_cn: 律师帮助了Jake。三天后，公司说："好，不涨租了。" Jake很开心，在群里发消息："4B刚刚救了我的公寓！🎉"

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: Why didn't people help Ryan before?

steps.speaker: Jake
steps.text: Because back then, we didn't have you.
steps.text_cn: 因为那时候，我们还没有你。

steps.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
steps.answer: You're the only person I can hear.
steps.normalized_answer: youre the only person i can hear
steps.feedback: Well done! 'Person' is for one — 'people' is for many. / 做得好！'Person' 指一个人——'people' 指很多人。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'The only person' = one person. Use 'person' here, not 'people'. / 'The only person' = 唯一一个人。这里用 'person'，不用 'people'。
steps.review_word: You're the only person I can hear.

steps.instruction: 请跟读：Tell me what happened.（重音：Tell / happened；语速：慢速，语气关心、耐心）
steps.feedback: Clear and caring! You sound like a trusted friend. / 清晰又关心！你听起来像一个值得信赖的朋友。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Try again: TELL me what HAPPENED. Stress 'tell' and 'happened'. / 再试一次：重读TELL和HAPPENED。
